name: Alto Sil Race Route
name_original: Laguna San Pedro Mallo, Primout y Columpio de Librán – circular por El Bierzo
description: The Alto Sil Race Route is a difficult 19.3-mile mountain bike trail in Alto Sil, featuring Laguna de San Pedro Mallo and Librán Swing.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Toreno are characterized by varied terrain, including forested paths, river valleys, and elevated sections offering panoramic views. The region presents a mix of challenging climbs and flowing descents suitable for experienced riders. Mountain biking routes often traverse through natural landscapes, providing diverse riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Toreno The most popular mountain bike trail is Alto Sil Race Route, a 19.3 miles (31.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and technical sections. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Dehesa Nueva loop from Cubillos del Sil, a moderate 26.7 miles (43.0 km) path. This route offers a balanced mix of climbs and descents through scenic landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Librán Swing – La Gradina Trail loop from Toreno, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail leading through varied forest and open terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Toreno is defined by forested paths, river valleys, and elevated sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging, difficult routes. The routes in Toreno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 8 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Toreno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Toreno?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Toreno, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate loops to challenging, difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Toreno?
A: Mountain bike trails around Toreno are characterized by varied terrain, including forested paths, river valleys, and elevated sections that offer panoramic views. Riders can expect a mix of challenging climbs and flowing descents, with some routes featuring technical sections.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Toreno?
A: Yes, Toreno offers several easier mountain bike trails. Out of the over 60 routes, 7 are classified as easy, providing a good starting point for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Toreno?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge will find 14 difficult routes around Toreno. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036652002/alto-sil-race-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Sil Race Route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.3-mile trail with significant elevation gain and technical sections. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31894552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Librán Swing – La Gradina Trail loop from Toreno&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.0-mile route through varied forest and open terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Toreno?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Toreno are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Dehesa Nueva loop from Cubillos del Sil&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.7-mile path offering a balanced mix of climbs and descents. Another example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31894552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Librán Swing – La Gradina Trail loop from Toreno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Toreno?
A: While mountain biking around Toreno, you can encounter various natural features and landmarks. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Catoute (2,117 m)&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zofreral de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; forest. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6183733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Dam on the Sil River&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Vega de Espinareda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about riding in Toreno?
A: The mountain bike trails in Toreno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 8 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which includes forested paths, river valleys, and elevated sections, providing diverse and rewarding riding experiences.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great viewpoints in Toreno?
A: Yes, many routes in Toreno include elevated sections that provide panoramic views of the surrounding natural landscapes. While specific viewpoints are not detailed for every trail, the varied terrain ensures opportunities for scenic vistas, especially on routes with significant elevation gain like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the River loop from Vega de Espinareda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Toreno?
A: While the guide highlights routes for experienced riders, Toreno does offer 7 easy trails that could be suitable for families or those with less experience. These routes typically have less elevation gain and technical difficulty, making them more accessible.

Q: Can I find trails that follow river valleys in Toreno?
A: Yes, the mountain bike trails around Toreno frequently traverse river valleys. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31444565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Spring on the Trail – Cúa River in Cacabelos loop from Vega de Espinareda&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that follows the Cúa River, offering scenic riverside riding.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Toreno?
A: Elevation gain varies significantly across trails in Toreno. Moderate routes might have around 500-600 meters of ascent, while difficult trails can feature substantial climbs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036652002/alto-sil-race-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Sil Race Route&lt;/a&gt; with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31894552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Librán Swing – La Gradina Trail loop from Toreno&lt;/a&gt; with over 1,400 meters.
tips: Located in an environment of pure nature and tranquility, the small lagoon of San Pedro Mallo is an ideal place to relax and follow a very pleasant and relaxed itinerary. Very close to here is the recreational area with everything you need to have a picnic if you prefer.
Wide dirt road near the town of Primout. If we do it from there, a beautiful climb towards Collada del Cancerbeiro awaits us.

The Librán Columpio is an impressive attraction located in the municipality of Toreno, in the province of León, Spain. It was inaugurated in July 2022 and has become a true tourist attraction for the area.
The swing was designed with the minimum use of material, prioritizing integration into the landscape and complying with all safety criteria for its use.
There is a circular route that starts from the town of Librán and crosses a place with spectacular views, passing through tunnels excavated in the rock and near the cave paintings of Librán.
In short, the Librán Swing is an exciting experience that combines nature, adventure and panoramic views. Definitely worth a visit! 😊🌟
Picturesque single track path through the small forest of La Gradina. Located between the popular swing of Librán and the town of the same name. A good choice for mountain bike lovers.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gain and technical sections. It's best suited for experienced mountain bikers who are comfortable with challenging terrain and sustained climbs.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a variety of terrain, including dirt roads, as highlighted by the Dirt Road to Primout, and potentially more technical singletrack sections. Given its difficulty rating, expect some rocky or uneven surfaces typical of mountain biking in mountainous regions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable highlights. You'll pass by the scenic Laguna de San Pedro Mallo, ride along the Dirt Road to Primout, and discover the unique Librán Swing. There's also a section on the La Gradina Trail.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the route?
faqs.answer: On average, mountain bikers can expect to spend around 3 hours and 33 minutes to complete this route. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the views or highlights.

faqs.question: Is the Alto Sil Race Route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, due to its difficult rating, significant elevation gain, and technical sections, this route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a good level of fitness and mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring snow or icy conditions, making the trail impassable or more hazardous.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route are not detailed, generally in natural areas like Alto Sil, dogs are permitted but should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Alto Sil region?
faqs.answer: Specific permits or entrance fees for mountain biking this particular route within the Alto Sil region are not commonly required for general recreational use. However, it's always wise to check local regulations or park authority websites for any updates before your visit, especially if participating in organized events.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead parking area isn't detailed, routes of this nature often start and end in or near villages. Look for designated parking in nearby towns or consult local maps for suitable access points. Planning your start point in advance is recommended.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the 'Carrera Alto Sil' for about 26% of the route, and also on sections of 'Camino a Primout' at various points.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate protective gear. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended, as specific signage might be limited.
